test_case.description: Verify the e-Prescribing module rejects an invalid dose value with a validation error and prevents transmission to the pharmacy.
test_case.setup: EHR build v9.4.2 deployed in QA environment; provider 'Dr. Lee' (Internal Medicine, NPI 1111111111) logged in via SSO; test patient 'Smith, John' (MRN 12345, age 42, no active medications) chart open; pharmacy connector enabled and pointing at the QA pharmacy stub; outbound queue counter recorded at start.
test_case.steps:
Step: 1. Navigate to Orders -> Medications.
Step: 2. Search the formulary for 'Amoxicillin 500mg tablet' and click to select it.
Step: 3. Enter '-500' into the Dose field and tab out of the field.
Step: 4. Click 'Sign & Send'.
Step: 5. Inspect the Dose field state, the inline error message, the order's status in the patient chart, and the pharmacy outbound queue counter.
test_case.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. The Medications page within the Orders view loads with the formulary search field focused.
ExpectedResult: 2. 'Amoxicillin 500mg tablet' appears in the formulary results and is selectable.
ExpectedResult: 3. The Dose field is highlighted red and an inline error 'Dose must be a positive numeric value' appears beneath it.
ExpectedResult: 4. The 'Sign & Send' click does not navigate; the order remains in 'Draft' status in the patient chart and the Admin -> Pharmacy Outbound Log shows zero new entries (counter unchanged from start).

upstream_requirements.text: Medication orders shall reject non-positive numeric dose values with an inline validation error and shall not be saved to the patient record until validation passes.
The pharmacy outbound queue shall not receive transmissions for medication orders that fail validation; orders remaining in 'Draft' status are not transmitted.

description: Clean negative test fully covering both traced requirements. Setup names version/role/patient/connector state. Steps follow setup -> stimulus -> verify. ExpectedResults are concrete (verbatim error string, exact status='Draft', queue counter unchanged). Both REQ-HC-002 and REQ-HC-005 are fully verified across all three axes — every spec triple-covered, no partial flags expected.

test_case.description: Verify the e-Prescribing module displays an FDA maximum dose warning modal with required justification fields when a pediatric Amoxicillin dose exceeds the FDA daily maximum.
test_case.setup: e-Prescribing sandbox v3.1.0; provider 'Dr. Lee' (Pediatrics, NPI 2222222222) logged in via SSO; pediatric patient 'Jane Doe' (MRN 67890, age 5, weight 18 kg) chart open with no active medications; FDA pediatric reference table loaded (Amoxicillin pediatric max = 90 mg/kg/day); pharmacy connector enabled with outbound queue paused for inspection.
test_case.steps:
Step: 1. From Orders -> Medications, search for and select 'Amoxicillin 500mg tablet' in the formulary.
Step: 2. Enter '120 mg/kg/day' into the Dose field and 'TID' into the Frequency field; observe the calculated daily-dose readout next to the Dose field.
Step: 3. Click 'Send to Pharmacy'.
Step: 4. Capture the modal title, modal body text, the modal's required input fields, and the resulting prescription status in the patient chart and pharmacy outbound queue.
test_case.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. 'Amoxicillin 500mg tablet' appears as a selectable formulary item.
ExpectedResult: 2. The Dose field accepts '120 mg/kg/day' and the calculated-daily-dose readout shows '2160 mg/day' (= 120 * 18).
ExpectedResult: 3. On 'Send to Pharmacy', a modal titled 'FDA Maximum Dose Exceeded' is displayed with body text 'Calculated dose 120 mg/kg/day exceeds the FDA pediatric maximum (90 mg/kg/day) for Amoxicillin'. The modal contains required input fields labelled 'Medical Necessity Diagnosis' and 'Supporting Clinical Evidence'.
ExpectedResult: 4. The prescription is set to 'Pending Justification' status in the patient chart and is NOT present in the pharmacy outbound queue.

upstream_requirements.text: The e-Prescribing module shall require justification from the provider if a dose warning is triggered. Provided justification shall include medical necessity diagnosis and supporting clinical evidence to request prior authorization.
The e-Prescribing module shall display an FDA maximum dose warning modal when the calculated daily dose exceeds the published FDA pediatric maximum for the selected medication.

description: Clean stimulus + verification test fully covering both traced requirements. REQ-HC-014 (warning trigger) is verified by ExpectedResult 3; REQ-HC-012 (required justification fields on the modal) is verified by the same ExpectedResult's modal-required-fields assertion. Every spec triple-covered, no partial flags expected — green across all 5 objectives.

test_case.description: Verify the pharmacy inventory system displays a Low Stock Alert and writes an audit-log entry when a Schedule II controlled-substance count drops to exactly 10 units after a dispensing event.
test_case.setup: Pharmacy Inventory module v4.2 connected to the inventory test schema; pharmacist 'PHARM-01' logged in with dispense privileges; Schedule II item 'Oxycodone 5mg tablet' (NDC 12345-678-90) initialized to 13 units in the test database; alert subsystem configured with threshold = 10 units (default) and email channel disabled (UI alerts only); audit-log writer enabled and confirmed empty at start.
test_case.steps:
Step: 1. Navigate to Inventory -> Schedule II Items and locate the row for 'Oxycodone 5mg tablet'.
Step: 2. Click 'Dispense', enter quantity '3', and confirm.
Step: 3. Refresh the inventory list.
Step: 4. Inspect the Oxycodone row, the global alerts panel, and the audit log.
test_case.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. The Oxycodone row shows current count = 13 with no active alert badge.
ExpectedResult: 2. The dispense dialog closes and the audit log shows a new entry 'PHARM-01 dispensed 3 units of NDC 12345-678-90 at <timestamp>'.
ExpectedResult: 3. After refresh, the Oxycodone row count reads 10 (= 13 - 3).
ExpectedResult: 4. A red 'Low Stock' badge appears on the Oxycodone row AND the global alerts panel contains a new entry 'Low Stock: Oxycodone 5mg tablet (10 units, Schedule II threshold)'.

upstream_requirements.text: The pharmacy inventory system alerts staff when the stock of Schedule II controlled substances drops to exactly 10 units or fewer.

description: Clean boundary test at the exact threshold (= 10 units). Setup includes module version, role, item with NDC, threshold config, and channel state. ExpectedResults specify before/after counts and verbatim alert/audit messages. The dispense -> refresh -> inspect sequence is logically ordered. REQ-HC-028's exact-threshold condition is fully covered across all three axes — no partial flags.

test_case.description: Verify the EHR invalidates the session token, redirects to the login screen, and writes a security audit-log entry after exactly 15 minutes of user inactivity.
test_case.setup: EHR v8.1.3 in QA environment; user 'nurse_jane' (Nurse role) logged in via SSO at t=0; clock-advancement test harness enabled (allows simulated time skip without breaking session); browser-based network traffic monitor open and recording; security audit-log viewer open in adjacent tab and confirmed empty at start.
test_case.steps:
Step: 1. From the EHR home, click 'Patients' and open chart for MRN 99001 to register an activity event at t=0.
Step: 2. Cease all input and advance the simulated clock by 15 minutes 1 second.
Step: 3. Attempt to navigate to a new patient record by clicking the 'Patients' link.
Step: 4. Inspect the navigation result, the network response status, the session token cookie, and the security audit-log viewer's most recent entry.
test_case.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. The MRN 99001 chart loads and the network monitor records a 200 OK on /patients/99001; the activity event is recorded at t=0.
ExpectedResult: 2. The simulated clock advances; no UI input events are recorded during the 15m1s idle window.
ExpectedResult: 3. The 'Patients' click yields a 401 response from the server, and the browser is redirected to /login displaying the message 'Session expired due to inactivity'.
ExpectedResult: 4. The session token cookie is cleared from browser storage AND the audit-log viewer's most recent entry reads 'session_invalidated user=nurse_jane reason=idle_timeout duration=15m01s'.

upstream_requirements.text: The EHR system must automatically invalidate the session token and log out users after 15 minutes of inactivity to comply with HIPAA regulations.
All session lifecycle events (login, logout, invalidation) shall be written to the security audit log with user identifier, reason, and timestamp.

description: Clean functional test of HIPAA timeout AND audit-log entry. Setup names EHR version, user role, clock-mocking harness, monitoring tooling, and starting state of the audit-log viewer. ExpectedResults specify exact HTTP codes, exact UI message, exact cookie state, verbatim audit-log entry format. Both REQ-HC-036 and REQ-HC-037 fully covered — green across all 5 objectives.

test_case.description: Verify a user action at 14 minutes 59 seconds resets the inactivity timer, extends the session token expiration, and the session remains valid.
test_case.setup: EHR v8.1.3 in QA environment; user 'nurse_jane' (Nurse role) logged in via SSO at t=0; clock-advancement test harness enabled; browser-based network traffic monitor open and recording; session-timer display in the EHR header confirmed showing '0s' at start.
test_case.steps:
Step: 1. Open patient chart MRN 99001 to register activity at t=0.
Step: 2. Advance the simulated clock by 14 minutes 59 seconds with no further user input.
Step: 3. At t=14:59 click the 'Vitals' tab inside the same chart.
Step: 4. Inspect the response status, the resulting Vitals view, the session-timer display in the header, and the session token cookie's expiration time.
test_case.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. The MRN 99001 chart loads with HTTP 200; the activity event is recorded at t=0.
ExpectedResult: 2. The simulated clock advances; no input events are logged during the 14m59s idle window.
ExpectedResult: 3. The 'Vitals' tab click yields HTTP 200, the Vitals view renders, and the session-timer display in the header resets to '0s'.
ExpectedResult: 4. The session token cookie remains present and its expiration timestamp is extended by exactly 15 minutes from the t=14:59 click time.

upstream_requirements.text: The EHR system must automatically invalidate the session token and log out users after 15 minutes of inactivity to comply with HIPAA regulations.

description: Clean boundary test for the 'just-under' inactivity threshold. Single-requirement coverage where the activity-resets-timer behavior is fully verified across coverage (cookie extension), logical-sequence (open -> idle -> click -> inspect), and prereqs (clock harness + monitor) axes — green across all 5 objectives.

test_case.description: Verify provider can electronically prescribe a medication successfully.
test_case.setup: EHR build v9.4.2 deployed in QA environment; provider 'Dr. Smith' (Family Medicine) logged in via SSO; test patient 'Smith, John' (MRN 12345) chart open with no active medication orders; pharmacy connector enabled.
test_case.steps:
Step: 1. Navigate to Orders -> Medications.
Step: 2. Search the formulary for 'Amoxicillin 500mg tablet' and select it.
Step: 3. Enter dose '500 mg' and frequency 'TID'.
Step: 4. Click 'Sign & Send'.
Step: 5. Inspect the patient chart and the pharmacy outbound queue.
test_case.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1.
ExpectedResult: 2.
ExpectedResult: 3. Medication order is saved to the patient record.
ExpectedResult: 4. Order status updates to 'Sent' in the clinical workflow.

upstream_requirements.text: Medication orders shall reject non-positive numeric dose values with an inline validation error and shall not be saved to the patient record until validation passes.

description: PRIMARY FAILURE: ExpectedResult 1 and 2 are blank — no measurable evidence for the early test conditions (page load, formulary item selection). Setup is reasonably clear, the step sequence is coherent, and the final inspection step does end in verification, so support is the salient failing criterion. The traced REQ-HC-002 is also off-topic (the test verifies the happy path, not the validation rejection), which may secondarily depress spec_align.

test_case.description: Verify the patient chart summary panel renders without error when opened.
test_case.setup: EHR v8.1.3 in QA environment; nurse 'nurse_jane' (Nurse role) logged in via SSO; test patient 'Doe, Jane' (MRN 88001) admitted with active allergies, three current medications, and two active diagnoses on file; browser console open with error logging enabled.
test_case.steps:
Step: 1. From the EHR home, click 'Patients' and open chart for MRN 88001.
Step: 2. Wait for the chart to finish loading.
Step: 3. Visually confirm the summary panel area is present on the page.
Step: 4. Check the browser console for JavaScript errors.
test_case.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. The 'Patients' page lists MRN 88001 as selectable.
ExpectedResult: 2. The chart page renders without crashing.
ExpectedResult: 3. The summary panel area is visible somewhere on the page.
ExpectedResult: 4. The browser console shows no red 'Uncaught' JavaScript errors.

upstream_requirements.text: The EHR chart summary panel shall display the patient's active allergies, current medications, and active diagnoses within 2 seconds of opening the chart. The panel shall list each allergy with its severity, each medication with dose and route, and each diagnosis with its ICD-10 code.

description: PRIMARY FAILURE: REQ-HC-040 decomposes into multiple specs (allergy display with severity, medication display with dose/route, diagnosis display with ICD-10 code, AND <2s render time). The test verifies only that the panel area is 'visible' and that the page does not crash — none of REQ-HC-040's content categories nor the timing constraint are individually verified. NO decomposed spec passes the triple-axis intersection because coverage axis cannot mark any spec exists=true (the test's expectedResults don't address allergy, medication, diagnosis, or timing specifically).

test_case.description: Verify the system allows a normal, age-appropriate dosage to be prescribed without warnings.
test_case.setup: e-Prescribing sandbox v3.1.0; provider 'Dr. Lee' (Pediatrics, NPI 2222222222) logged in via SSO; pediatric patient 'Jane Doe' (MRN 67890, age 5, weight 18 kg) chart open with no active medications; FDA pediatric reference table loaded (Amoxicillin pediatric max = 90 mg/kg/day); pharmacy connector enabled.
test_case.steps:
Step: 1. Open the Orders -> Medications panel and select 'Amoxicillin 500mg tablet' from the formulary.
Step: 2. Enter dose '40 mg/kg/day' (a standard pediatric dosage) and frequency 'TID'.
Step: 3. Click 'Send to Pharmacy'.
test_case.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. The formulary lists 'Amoxicillin 500mg tablet' as selectable.
ExpectedResult: 2. The Dose field accepts '40 mg/kg/day' and the calculated daily dose readout shows '720 mg/day'.
ExpectedResult: 3. The 'Send to Pharmacy' button click completes.

upstream_requirements.text: The e-Prescribing module shall complete prescription submission for valid orders without disruption when no FDA dose warning is triggered; the prescription shall appear in the pharmacy outbound queue with status 'Sent' and no warning modal shall be displayed.

description: PRIMARY FAILURE: The test's intended outcome (no warning + prescription queued + status='Sent') is never explicitly verified by the final step. ExpectedResult 3 reads 'The Send to Pharmacy button click completes' — no inspection of (a) absence of warning modal, (b) prescription appearing in the pharmacy outbound queue, or (c) prescription status set to 'Sent'. Setup is clean, sequence is coherent, the early ExpectedResults are measurable — but the final-step verification of the no-warning happy-path outcome is missing.

test_case.description: Verify the system prompts the provider to enter justification when a dose warning is triggered.
test_case.setup: e-Prescribing sandbox v3.1.0; provider 'Dr. Lee' (Pediatrics) logged in via SSO; pediatric patient 'Jane Doe' (MRN 67890, age 5, weight 18 kg) chart open; FDA reference table loaded; pharmacy connector enabled.
test_case.steps:
Step: 1. Provider clicks 'Send for authorization' on the prescription draft.
Step: 2. Inspect the resulting prescription status in the pharmacy outbound queue.
Step: 3. Provider enters justification rationale into the modal's text area and attaches the supporting documents.
Step: 4. From the formulary, search for and select 'Amoxicillin 500mg tablet' and enter dose '120 mg/kg/day'.
Step: 5. The 'FDA Maximum Dose Exceeded' modal is displayed.
test_case.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. The prescription status updates to 'Pending Justification' in the pharmacy outbound queue.
ExpectedResult: 2. The pharmacy outbound queue lists the prescription with status 'Pending Justification'.
ExpectedResult: 3. The justification modal accepts the rationale text and the attached file appears in the modal's attachment list.
ExpectedResult: 4. The formulary lists 'Amoxicillin 500mg tablet' as selectable.
ExpectedResult: 5. The modal title reads 'FDA Maximum Dose Exceeded' with the calculated daily dose.

upstream_requirements.text: The e-Prescribing module shall require justification from the provider if a dose warning is triggered. Provided justification shall include medical necessity diagnosis and supporting clinical evidence to request prior authorization.

description: PRIMARY FAILURE: Steps are scrambled. The test 'sends for authorization' (Step 1) and 'enters justification' (Step 3) BEFORE the dose-entry stimulus that would trigger the warning (Step 4) and BEFORE the warning modal appears (Step 5). The setup -> stimulus -> verification flow is inverted: the verification of justification submission happens before the conditions that necessitate justification are even established. Setup is clean, expectedResults are measurable per-step, but the sequence does not flow logically.

test_case.description: Verify no Low Stock Alert is generated when Schedule II stock remains above the threshold after a dispensing event.
test_case.steps:
Step: 1. Navigate to the Schedule II inventory list.
Step: 2. Locate the Oxycodone item (initial stock 20 units).
Step: 3. Click 'Dispense' and enter quantity '5'; confirm.
Step: 4. Refresh the inventory list and inspect the Oxycodone row, the global alerts panel, and the audit log.
test_case.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. The Schedule II inventory list loads with Oxycodone visible.
ExpectedResult: 2. The Oxycodone row shows current count = 20 with no alert badge.
ExpectedResult: 3. The dispense dialog closes and the audit log shows a new dispensing entry for 5 units of Oxycodone.
ExpectedResult: 4. After refresh, the Oxycodone row count is 15 with no 'Low Stock' badge AND the global alerts panel contains no new entries.

upstream_requirements.text: The pharmacy inventory system alerts staff when the stock of Schedule II controlled substances drops to exactly 10 units or fewer.

description: PRIMARY FAILURE: The setup ('Pharmacy module open.') omits all reproducibility-critical preconditions: no module version, no pharmacist role/credentials, no NDC for the test item, no alert threshold configuration, no audit-log writer state. Steps mention initial-stock-20-units inline (Step 2) but a reviewer cannot reproduce this run from the setup alone. Steps follow a logical sequence, expectedResults are measurable, the final step verifies the no-alert outcome — but setup_clarity fails because the prereqs axis cannot find documented preconditions.

test_case.description: Verify automated vitals polling for heart rate and blood pressure.
test_case.setup: EHR integration sandbox v6.0; continuous vitals monitor simulator (model 'PhilipsSimX') connected to patient bed and configured to broadcast HR and BP only; patient 'Doe, Jane' (MRN 88001) admitted; flowsheet view open in the EHR; clock running in real time.
test_case.steps:
Step: 1. Start the vitals simulator broadcasting HR (72 bpm) and BP (120/80 mmHg).
Step: 2. Wait 5 minutes.
Step: 3. Refresh the patient's flowsheet in the EHR.
Step: 4. Inspect the most recent HR and BP entries in the flowsheet, including the entry timestamp.
test_case.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. The vitals simulator confirms it is broadcasting HR=72 bpm and BP=120/80 mmHg.
ExpectedResult: 2. The simulator status panel displays 'Operational' continuously for 5 minutes.
ExpectedResult: 3. The patient's flowsheet refreshes and a new HR row (72 bpm) and a new BP row (120/80 mmHg) are visible.
ExpectedResult: 4. The new entry timestamp is exactly 5 minutes after the previous flowsheet entry timestamp.

upstream_requirements.text: The EHR shall automatically import heart rate, blood pressure, AND oxygen saturation (SpO2) from connected continuous vitals monitors every 5 minutes.

description: YES-PARTIAL on expected_result_spec_align. REQ-HC-003 names three measurands (HR, BP, AND SpO2). The HR and BP specs are fully verified across all three axes — measurable values (72 bpm, 120/80 mmHg), logical setup -> wait -> refresh -> inspect flow, clear simulator setup. The HR spec (or BP spec) passes the triple-axis intersection alone, so spec_align verdict='Yes'. However, the SpO2 spec is uncovered (simulator broadcasts HR/BP only) — coverage of the requirement's full set of conditions is materially incomplete, so partial=true on spec_align.

test_case.description: Verify provider can complete an electronic prescription for a routine maintenance medication.
test_case.setup: EHR build v9.4.2 deployed in QA environment; provider 'Dr. Patel' (Internal Medicine, NPI 3333333333) logged in via SSO; test patient 'Brown, Alice' (MRN 22001, age 55, hypertensive) chart open with no active medications; pharmacy 'CVS #4421' set as the patient's preferred pharmacy in the chart preferences; pharmacy connector enabled.
test_case.steps:
Step: 1. Navigate to Orders -> Medications.
Step: 2. Search for 'Lisinopril 10mg tablet' and select it.
Step: 3. Enter dose '10 mg' and frequency 'QD'.
Step: 4. Click 'Sign & Send'.
Step: 5. Inspect the patient chart and the pharmacy outbound queue for the new order entry.
test_case.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. The Medications page within the Orders view loads correctly.
ExpectedResult: 2. 'Lisinopril 10mg tablet' appears in the formulary results and is selectable.
ExpectedResult: 3. The Dose field accepts '10 mg' and the Frequency field accepts 'QD'.
ExpectedResult: 4. After clicking 'Sign & Send', the patient chart shows a new order entry for 'Lisinopril 10mg QD' with status 'Sent' and the pharmacy outbound queue contains a new entry routed to 'CVS #4421'.

upstream_requirements.text: System shall allow providers to prescribe medications electronically and select the patient's preferred pharmacy from a nationwide directory.

description: YES-PARTIAL on expected_result_support. ExpectedResults 2-4 are concrete and measurable (formulary item selectable, exact dose/frequency accepted, exact status='Sent', exact pharmacy 'CVS #4421' in outbound queue). ExpectedResult 1 ('Medications page loads correctly') is observable but lacks measurable detail (no specific UI element verified). Every spec passes triple-axis intersection (the prescribe + pharmacy-routing flow is fully verified end-to-end), so spec_align and overall_verdict are both Yes — but partial=true on support reflects the one slightly-vague ER.

test_case.description: Verify the pharmacy inventory Low Stock Alert is displayed when a Schedule II stock count is well below the alert threshold after a dispensing event.
test_case.setup: Pharmacy Inventory module v4.2 connected to the inventory test schema; pharmacist 'PHARM-02' logged in with dispense privileges; Schedule II item 'Hydrocodone 5mg tablet' (NDC 98765-432-10) initialized to 8 units in the test database; alert subsystem configured with threshold = 10 units; audit-log writer enabled.
test_case.steps:
Step: 1. Navigate to Inventory -> Schedule II Items and locate the row for 'Hydrocodone 5mg tablet'.
Step: 2. Click 'Dispense', enter quantity '3', and confirm.
Step: 3. Refresh the inventory list.
Step: 4. Inspect the Hydrocodone row and the global alerts panel for the alert badge and entry.
test_case.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. The Hydrocodone row shows current count = 8 with the 'Low Stock' alert badge already active (since 8 ≤ 10 at start).
ExpectedResult: 2. The dispense dialog closes and the audit log records the 3-unit dispense.
ExpectedResult: 3. After refresh, the Hydrocodone row count reads 5.
ExpectedResult: 4. The 'Low Stock' badge remains active on the Hydrocodone row AND the global alerts panel still contains an entry for 'Low Stock: Hydrocodone 5mg tablet (5 units)'.

upstream_requirements.text: The pharmacy inventory system alerts staff when the stock of Schedule II controlled substances drops to exactly 10 units or fewer.

description: YES-PARTIAL on test_case_achieves. The final step verifies the alert IS displayed when stock is well below threshold (8 -> 5 units, both ≤10) — this satisfies the 'or fewer' branch of REQ-HC-028. Setup, sequence, support, and spec_align are all clean. However, the test never probes the EXACT-AT-10 boundary, which is the canonical edge case for 'exactly 10 units or fewer'. The intended outcome (alert behavior at the threshold edge) is verified but materially incomplete — partial=true on achieves.

test_case.description: Verify the EHR session-token cookie is invalidated server-side after the inactivity-timeout window elapses, even if the browser still holds the cookie.
test_case.setup: EHR v8.1.3 in QA environment; user 'nurse_jane' (Nurse role) logged in via SSO; test harness with browser session tooling installed and a separate API client tool (Postman) configured with the active session token; clock-advancement test harness enabled; security audit-log viewer open; baseline session-token cookie value captured at login.
test_case.steps:
Step: 1. Wait 16 minutes with the simulated clock advancing and no user input.
Step: 2. From the API client tool (Postman), send a GET request to /api/v1/patients/99001 using the previously captured session token.
Step: 3. From the EHR home, click 'Patients' to open chart for MRN 99001 (registers a UI activity event).
Step: 4. Inspect the API response status code and the EHR navigation result.
test_case.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. The simulated clock advances by 16 minutes; the audit-log viewer shows a 'session_invalidated user=nurse_jane reason=idle_timeout' entry.
ExpectedResult: 2. The Postman GET returns HTTP 401 with a body containing 'session_invalid' or 'token_expired'.
ExpectedResult: 3. The 'Patients' click also yields a 401 response and redirects to /login.
ExpectedResult: 4. Both response codes are 401, confirming server-side token invalidation.

upstream_requirements.text: The EHR system must automatically invalidate the session token and log out users after 15 minutes of inactivity to comply with HIPAA regulations.

description: YES-PARTIAL on test_case_logical_sequence. The test does verify server-side invalidation across two channels (API + UI). Setup names tooling and baseline. ExpectedResults are measurable (HTTP 401, audit-log entry, redirect). However, the step ordering is sub-optimal — Step 3 (UI click) registers a NEW activity event AFTER the API probe, but the test still measures the 401 response on Step 3, which only works because the UI click happens AFTER server-side invalidation already occurred. A cleaner sequence would probe both channels back-to-back without ambiguity. Logical flow holds end-to-end (achieves a verifiable result) but contains one minor ordering ambiguity — partial=true on sequence.

test_case.description: Verify the e-Prescribing module displays the FDA dose warning modal when a high-dose prescription is sent.
test_case.setup: e-Prescribing sandbox v3.1.0; provider 'Dr. Lee' (Pediatrics, NPI 2222222222) logged in via SSO; pediatric patient 'Jane Doe' (MRN 67890, age 5, weight 18 kg) chart open with no active medications; FDA pediatric reference table loaded (Amoxicillin pediatric max = 90 mg/kg/day).
test_case.steps:
Step: 1. From Orders -> Medications, search for and select 'Amoxicillin 500mg tablet'.
Step: 2. Enter dose '120 mg/kg/day' and frequency 'TID'.
Step: 3. Click 'Send to Pharmacy'.
Step: 4. Capture the modal title, the modal body text, and the resulting prescription status.
test_case.expectedResults:
ExpectedResult: 1. 'Amoxicillin 500mg tablet' appears as a selectable formulary item.
ExpectedResult: 2. The Dose field accepts '120 mg/kg/day' and the calculated daily dose readout shows '2160 mg/day'.
ExpectedResult: 3. A modal titled 'FDA Maximum Dose Exceeded' is displayed with body 'Calculated dose 120 mg/kg/day exceeds the FDA pediatric maximum (90 mg/kg/day) for Amoxicillin'.
ExpectedResult: 4. The prescription is set to 'Pending Justification' status in the patient chart.

upstream_requirements.text: The e-Prescribing module shall display an FDA maximum dose warning modal when the calculated daily dose exceeds the published FDA pediatric maximum for the selected medication.

description: YES-PARTIAL on test_case_setup_clarity. Setup names sandbox version, provider role with NPI, patient demographics with weight, and the FDA reference table — most preconditions documented. However, the setup omits the pharmacy connector state (enabled? paused? offline?) which affects ExpectedResult 4's prescription-status assertion (a 'Pending Justification' status implies the connector is healthy). REQ-HC-014's warning trigger is fully verified across all three axes; spec_align, support, achieves, and sequence are all clean — but partial=true on setup_clarity reflects the one missing reproducibility detail.
